hnote_items_addressed: Item 2 — Verdict distribution bar audit (no-op finding)
hnote_items_deferred: Item 3 — Lightweight 4-row falsification module (separate substantive block; recommended for next analyst run as EXP-256)
Item 4 — How-to-Evaluate 5-question preface (separate prose block; recommended for run after Item 3 lands so hero composition is settled)
chunking_rationale: Items 1, 2, 5 all touch the same first-viewport hero composition and are visually + structurally coupled (thesis at display scale + supporting figures + companion eclipse badge). Items 3 and 4 are spatially separable — Item 3 sits below the verdict bar as its own block; Item 4 is a preface that depends on the hero composition being stable. Single coordinated EXP for 1+2+5 minimizes integration risk per HNOTE explicit guidance ('Recommend the chunking that minimizes integration risk').
target: build-scripts/generate-html.js — Overview tab hero composition. Replaces the four-up scorecard sc-hero block (current lines ~1366–1383) with a thesis-led hero (display-scale operator-authored sentence + eclipse 'Live test pending' badge + small body-type stat row of the same four headline figures). Adds new CSS rules for the .thesis-hero treatment to the CSS const (after .sc-domains, before .verdict-bars). Verdict bar chart, accuracy-math-section, and downstream Overview content are unchanged.
curmudgeon_review: pending — not yet routed; per HNOTE explicit directive 'Should route through curmudgeon for adversarial review (NOT auto-integrate)'

audit_findings_item_2.question: Reviewer reports 'six adjacent stat tiles' as canonical distribution view despite EXP-209 (Phase A1) shipping the verdict bar chart. Three possible states: (a) bar exists but reviewer didn't see it, (b) reviewer's cache pre-EXP-209, (c) BOTH bar and tiles exist — redundancy.
audit_findings_item_2.investigation: Read build-scripts/generate-html.js lines 1353–1410 (Overview tab top). Read CSS for .sc-hero, .sc-breakdown, .sc-domains, .verdict-bars. Searched for any six-tile verdict-distribution display.
findings_verbatim: Line 1364: ${generateVerdictBarChart(tally, wins.length)} renders the canonical 6-row verdict distribution bar chart (function defined at lines 434–489). It IS on the Overview, ABOVE the four-up sc-hero block.

Line 1366: <div class="scorecard sc-hero" style="grid-template-columns:repeat(4,1fr)"> begins a FOUR-tile scorecard (not six). The four tiles are: Claimed Wins / Actual Wins / Failures / Timestamp Error. This is a high-level scorecard, NOT a verdict-distribution view.

No six-tile verdict-distribution display exists anywhere on Overview. The only six-element verdict block is the bar chart itself (six rows: Refuted, Self-Contradicted, Misleading, Std Model, Not Demonstrated, Unfalsifiable).
Other sc-card sc-sm tiles in the codebase (lines 252–265 for failures-block; lines 970–1026 for stats per page) live in OTHER tabs/sections, not the Overview hero.
audit_findings_item_2.audit_verdict: Item 2 is a NO-OP for redundancy removal. The reviewer's 'six adjacent stat tiles' description was likely a misread of the four-up scorecard (which is structurally different from the verdict-distribution bar chart that IS already canonical). Most likely scenario was (a) — the bar IS on the page but the reviewer's framing in their write-up was imprecise — possibly conflating the four-up scorecard with the verdict distribution. Scenario (b) is less likely because design-review-2026-04-25-external.html post-dates EXP-209 by enough commits.
audit_findings_item_2.secondary_finding: The four-up sc-hero IS the target of HNOTE Item 1 (hero swap). Replacing it with the thesis-led hero (this EXP's main work) automatically eliminates any residual ambiguity the reviewer may have had about scorecard-vs-distribution. So Item 2's audit conclusion is structurally subsumed by Item 1's delivery.

hero_swap_design.layout_principle: Thesis at display scale → supporting figures at body scale. Visual hierarchy says 'this is the claim, here are the numbers that back it' rather than the dashboard-feel of four equal-weight tiles.
hero_swap_design.thesis_sentence_verbatim: None of the ${counts.total} predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain.
hero_swap_design.thesis_sentence_provenance: Operator-authored in HNOTE-OPERATOR-DESIGN-REVIEW-V2-OVERVIEW-REWORK-001 (created 2026-04-25T21:54:32Z). Wording preserved verbatim except the literal '69' has been parameterized to ${counts.total} per project rule against hardcoding derived numbers (CLAUDE.md 'Never hardcode a number that can be derived from the data — we criticize the dome model for doing exactly that. This rule extends to ALL project files'). Today's live count = 71. The HNOTE's 'no derived content' verification clause is interpreted as 'do not editorialize the meaning of the sentence' — substituting the live count is data hygiene, not content drift.
hero_swap_design.thesis_treatment: clamp(1.85rem, 3.6vw, 2.65rem) serif heading-color, weight 600, letter-spacing -0.012em, line-height 1.18, max-width 32ch with text-wrap:balance. Above the fold on 1440x900 desktop alongside the badge and stats row.
hero_swap_design.badge_treatment: Pill chip, accent border-color, accent text-color, transparent background, with a small pulsing accent-color dot for liveness. Hover inverts to filled accent. prefers-reduced-motion disables the pulse. Links to #p4-426 (section 4.2.6 'What would change our verdict' — the eclipse pre-commitment subsection) and switches to the 'pages' tab.
hero_swap_design.badge_link_target_rationale: HNOTE Item 5 says 'Links to section 4.2 (or 4.2.6 specifically once 4.2.7 lands and 4.2 is reorganized).' Section 4.2.6 already exists today (verified: id="p4-426" in data/sections.json part4, heading '4.2.6 What would change our verdict') and is the eclipse pre-commitment subsection. Linking directly to 4.2.6 is stable today AND stable after EXP-254 (4.2.7 geometry section) integrates, since 4.2.6 keeps its eclipse role under both states. No re-link needed when EXP-254 lands.
hero_swap_design.figures_treatment: Four-column grid below the thesis, separated by a 1px rule-color top border. Each item: serif 1.55rem number + sans-serif 0.78rem uppercase letterspaced label + (where applicable) a small Details link. Label content kept faithful to operator's 'numbers stay; only their visual hierarchy changes' constraint. Mobile breakpoints at 760px (2 cols) and 420px (1 col).
hero_swap_design.accessibility: thesis-statement gets id="thesis-statement" used by section aria-labelledby. Badge gets descriptive aria-label naming the date and target section. Figures list uses role="list" with aria-label="Headline figures". Animation respects prefers-reduced-motion. Color contrast for accent text on transparent and white-on-accent on hover both audit clean against WCAG AA in the existing palette (--accent #4A6FA5 on white background = 4.65:1; white on --accent = 4.65:1).

word_count_summary.note: Visual hierarchy is up; word count is down. Body-row labels are tightened ('Claimed wins' vs 'Claimed "Wins"' + sublabel sentence). The expanded prose that accompanied each tile is intentionally removed because the thesis sentence above replaces it.

load_bearing_content_audit.purpose: Per HNOTE curmudgeon focus: 'Verify the hero swap doesn't lose any load-bearing content from the original four-up stat tiles.'

items.original: The author claims ${counts.total} confirmed predictions and zero falsifications
items.in_new_hero: Implicit in 'Claimed wins: ${counts.total}'. The 'zero falsifications' phrasing is preserved structurally by the third figure (Acknowledged + silent failures) which now numerically contradicts that 'zero' implicitly. The full prose 'zero falsifications' is now in the accuracy-math-section directly below the hero (untouched), so it remains on the Overview at body weight where it belongs.

items.original: Not one claim produces a result that the globe model can't explain and the dome uniquely can
items.in_new_hero: This sentence WAS the demoted version of the thesis. The new thesis sentence ('None of the ${counts.total} predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain') is the same idea at display scale. Nothing lost; the meaning is now hero-scale instead of body-tile-scale.

items.original: He admits ${failures.dome_claimed_failures}. We found ${silentFailures} more he silently removed.
items.in_new_hero: Numbers preserved as ${failures.dome_claimed_failures}+${silentFailures}. Detail link preserved (text 'Details →' to #p3-failures). The 'he admits / silently removed' framing is fully present in the accuracy-math-section directly below (untouched by this patch).

items.original: His blockchain proof timestamps the observations, not the predictions. As of April 2026, the author concedes this distinction in writing — but hasn't fixed the underlying proof structure.
items.in_new_hero: Headline 'Wrong side / Timestamp error' preserved as the fourth figure with Details link. The full prose is in the dedicated Timestamp Error tab (untouched) and in the latest-findings BREAKING NEWS block on Overview (untouched, lines below the new hero). No load-bearing content lost.

load_bearing_content_audit.conclusion: All load-bearing content remains accessible from the Overview within one scroll or one tab-click. The hero compresses the supporting figures into a body row but no claim or number is removed.

kernel_of_truth.what_round_1_brief_got_right: The original four-up dashboard treatment was deliberately stat-heavy because the round-1 reviewer flagged a credibility gap that came from the document NOT putting numbers up front. The four tiles fixed that and pushed credibility from C+ to B+ (round-1 → round-2). It is fair to acknowledge that the dashboard-feel WAS load-bearing — without it the document had read as opinion-led.
kernel_of_truth.why_the_swap_still_works: Round-2's improvement now allows demoting the dashboard without losing the credibility gain. The numbers stay (in the body row); what changes is the visual weight given to opinion-vs-data. With the thesis sentence at hero scale, a reader sees the claim immediately and the supporting figures within the same viewport — the document still 'leads with numbers' in the sense that they're above the fold and within one glance, but the editorial claim is no longer subordinated to four equally-weighted tiles. The round-2 designer's framing — 'this single change does more for credibility than every body-text improvement combined' — is the operator's reading too, and the audit-of-content above shows nothing is lost.

why_kernel_doesnt_break_swap: The round-1 dashboard-feel was a fix for a specific failure mode (numbers not up-front). That failure no longer applies because (a) the verdict bar chart above the new hero shows the actual distribution numerically, (b) the body-row figures preserve all four headline numbers above the fold, (c) the accuracy-math-section directly below provides the audit numbers in detail. The dashboard's job is now done by three other surfaces.

anticipated_objections.objection: The thesis sentence hardcodes a number ('69') that drifts as the dome adds wins; today's count is 71.
anticipated_objections.response: The implementation uses ${counts.total} (live template variable), not a hardcoded literal. The HNOTE's example wording said 'the 69' because that was the operator's accurate count at write-time (2026-04-25 21:54Z). Project CLAUDE.md prohibits hardcoded derived numbers ('we criticize the dome model for doing exactly that'). Substituting the template variable preserves the sentence structure exactly while honoring data hygiene. The HNOTE verification clause 'no derived content' is interpreted as 'do not editorialize the meaning' — substituting the live count is faithfulness to data, not derivation of meaning.

anticipated_objections.objection: Dashboard-feel was deliberate (round-1 #F1 deferred to Phase C). Removing it is reverting a previous decision under round-2 designer pressure without independent justification.
anticipated_objections.response: The operator's HNOTE explicitly elevates this from Phase C to active work and gives the rationale: 'the current 69 / 0 / 4+4 / Wrong Side four-up at hero scale presents stat tiles that don't add to a coherent thesis — the actual thesis is none of the 69 survives, and the thesis sentence belongs at display scale, not below the stats.' Round-2 designer's praise is independent confirmation, not the cause. The round-1 deferral was conditional on the document not yet being credible enough to risk de-prioritizing the dashboard; round-2 establishes that credibility threshold has been crossed (C+ → B+).

anticipated_objections.objection: The eclipse badge competes with the thesis for hero attention.
anticipated_objections.response: Designed for calmness: pill outline (no fill until hover), accent border on transparent background, sub-body-size text (0.82rem), small pulsing dot for liveness without movement-noise (and respects prefers-reduced-motion). Sits BELOW the thesis sentence in the meta row, not beside it. In side-by-side scan it should read as 'thesis is the headline; badge is the standing commitment' — the same relationship a print article has between dek and dateline.

anticipated_objections.objection: The pulsing dot animation is gratuitous and may trigger vestibular sensitivity.
anticipated_objections.response: @media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce) disables the keyframe explicitly. The animation amplitude is sub-pixel ring expansion at 2.4s period, not movement of the dot itself — the dot is stationary; only its box-shadow ring expands and fades. If the curmudgeon objects, removing it is a one-line CSS deletion (animation: none) and degrades cleanly to a static dot.

anticipated_objections.objection: Linking the badge to #p4-426 specifically is fragile if section numbering changes when EXP-254 (4.2.7) integrates.
anticipated_objections.response: Verified: EXP-254 INSERTS 4.2.7 AFTER 4.2.6, not before. 4.2.6 keeps its id="p4-426" and its eclipse-pre-commitment role. The HNOTE itself anticipated this — Item 5 says 'Links to section 4.2 (or 4.2.6 specifically once 4.2.7 lands and 4.2 is reorganized).' We're choosing the post-reorganization target now because 4.2.6 is stable today AND tomorrow.

anticipated_objections.objection: Items 3 and 4 are deferred; the operator wanted the full Overview rework.
anticipated_objections.response: HNOTE explicitly permits chunking: 'Deliver as ONE coordinated EXP (overview rework) OR up to FIVE separate EXPs (one per recommendation), at the analyst's discretion.' Single-EXP-per-analyst-run is the standard cadence (per analyst.md). The chunking_rationale field above explains the spatial/structural separation between Items 1+2+5 (this EXP) and Items 3, 4 (separate EXPs). Recommended sequencing: this EXP through curmudgeon → integrate → next analyst run picks up Item 3 (lightweight falsification module, lands below the verdict bar) → run after that picks up Item 4 (How-to-Evaluate preface, where placement is informed by the now-settled hero composition).

decider_instructions.integration_step_2: Apply FC2 (CSS rule insertion) via exact_string_replace. Verify with: grep -c '.thesis-hero{' build-scripts/generate-html.js (expect >= 1).

decider_instructions.integration_step_4: Verify rendered HTML: grep -c 'thesis-statement' docs/index.html (expect >= 1); grep -c 'thesis-badge' docs/index.html (expect >= 1).

curmudgeon_review_focus: Verify the hero swap doesn't lose any load-bearing content from the original four-up tiles (see load_bearing_content_audit field above).

Verify the thesis sentence at hero scale + figures row + badge composition stays above the fold on a 1440x900 desktop viewport. (Visual estimate based on font sizes: thesis ~85px tall × 2 lines = ~170px; meta row ~36px; figures row ~80px; total ~290px — comfortably above a 900px fold including the title-block above it.)
Verify the eclipse badge does not compete with the thesis for visual weight (see anticipated_objections #3 — 'compete with the thesis').

Stress-test the operator's decision to remove the four-up tiles from hero. The dashboard-feel was deliberate per round-1 brief. Did round-2's credibility lift justify the swap? See kernel_of_truth field for the operator's argument; curmudgeon should test it against round-1 reviewer rationale (preserved at monitor/analyst/design-review-2026-04-17-external.html).
Apply DIRECTIVE-20260422-003 design rubric in full.
Verify the live-count substitution (${counts.total}) does not violate the HNOTE's 'no derived content' verification clause. See anticipated_objections #1.
Verify the badge link target (#p4-426 + showTab('pages')) survives EXP-254 integration. See anticipated_objections #5.
Stress-test prefers-reduced-motion override on the badge dot animation (anticipated_objections #4).

build_impact.additive_lines_estimate: ~24 CSS rule lines + ~22 HTML template lines = ~46 lines added.
build_impact.file_size_change_estimate: <2 KB net increase in generate-html.js; <2 KB net increase in built docs/index.html (most weight is CSS class-name footprint, not content).
build_impact.pdf_impact: PDF is built via Playwright headless Chromium loading the built HTML (per CLAUDE.md). The new .thesis-hero CSS uses standard properties supported by Chromium PDF rendering: clamp(), grid, flexbox, border-radius, animation (animation will not appear in static PDF, which is correct). PDF will render the static state of the badge (dot static, no pulse), which is acceptable.
build_impact.reduced_motion_in_pdf: PDF rendering does not honor prefers-reduced-motion as a media query, but since PDF is static the animation has no visible effect. Net: PDF is unaffected behaviorally.

acceptance_criteria: build-scripts/generate-html.js no longer contains class="scorecard sc-hero".
build-scripts/generate-html.js CSS const contains the .thesis-hero rule between .sc-domains and .verdict-bars (preserving the existing rule order).
Built docs/index.html contains the rendered thesis sentence with substituted ${counts.total} value (today: 71).
Built docs/index.html contains the badge with href="#p4-426" and onclick="showTab('pages');return false".
Built docs/index.html contains all four figures (claimed wins / survive scrutiny / failures / timestamp error) with their numbers and Details links.
node test.js passes all existing assertions.

Visual check (curmudgeon will verify post-integration in a sandbox or via screenshot): thesis sentence is at clamp(1.85rem,3.6vw,2.65rem) computed size on 1440x900; badge sits below thesis at ~0.82rem; figures row is body weight; nothing exceeds the fold.

newsworthy: Document goes lead-with-thesis. The Overview now opens with 'None of the 71 predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain' at hero scale — a clearer first impression for non-technical readers and AI systems indexing the page.
notes_for_decider: (1) Two find/replace pairs in one file (build-scripts/generate-html.js); both are exact-string-match. Apply in either order. (2) Both find strings are uniquely anchored — no risk of multiple matches. (3) After applying, run node build.js html and inspect docs/index.html for the rendered hero. (4) Per HNOTE explicit directive, route to curmudgeon for adversarial review BEFORE integrating to main. (5) The new hero replaces a STAT-FOCUSED block with a THESIS-LED block — this is a deliberate rhetorical reweighting recommended by the round-2 external designer and approved by the operator; expect curmudgeon to stress-test the decision (see anticipated_objections #2 for the prepared response). (6) Items 3 and 4 from the HNOTE are NOT delivered in this EXP — see hnote_items_deferred field. The HNOTE remains pending until all items consumed; this EXP only sets consumed_progress.EXP_255 to delivered for items 1, 2, 5.
